slides.title: FlowSense
slides.subtitle: Analyzes UX task-flow recordings and scores interaction quality against a 7-category rubric.
slides.narration: This is FlowSense. It scores the interaction quality of your product, automatically.

slides.title: Judging interaction quality is slow, subjective, and inconsistent.
slides.body: Teams watch recordings by eye and argue over what “good” actually feels like.
slides.narration: Today, judging interaction quality is slow and subjective. Teams watch recordings and argue over what good feels like.

slides.title: Built for the people reviewing real task flows.
slides.body: UX researchers, designers, and PMs who need evidence, not opinions.
slides.narration: It's built for UX researchers, designers, and PMs who need evidence, not opinions.

slides.title: Interaction quality is visible frame-by-frame.
slides.body: State changes across frames reveal feedback, friction, and dead clicks — so AI can score it.
slides.narration: Here's the insight: interaction quality is visible frame by frame. State changes reveal feedback, friction, and dead clicks. So AI can score it.

slides.title: Upload a recording. Get scores, issues, and fixes.
slides.body: Drop a screen recording and get a secure, rubric-based interaction-quality report.
slides.narration: So FlowSense turns any task-flow recording into scores, issues, and concrete fixes.

visual.alt: FlowSense — Analyze UX Flows with AI

slides.title: How it works.
slides.narration: It extracts keyframes from the recording, runs them through Azure OpenAI vision, and scores each category from zero to two.

slides.title: A 7-category interaction-quality rubric.
slides.narration: Every flow is scored across seven categories — from response integrity and system feedback to friction, error recovery, and efficiency.

slides.title: Azure-native and secure by default.

cards.heading: Entra ID auth
cards.text: Signed-in sessions required for every app and API route.

cards.heading: Managed identity
cards.text: Azure SQL and Blob via managed identity — no shared keys at runtime.

cards.heading: Signed webhooks
cards.text: Timestamp + nonce replay protection on processor webhooks.

slides.narration: It's Azure-native and secure by default: Entra sign-in, managed identity, and signed webhooks with replay protection.

slides.title: From debates to evidence.
bullets: Per-frame justifications cite real UI evidence
slides.narration: Every score is backed by per-frame evidence, checked for self-consistency, and benchmarked against human agreement.

slides.title: Stop guessing. Score the flow.
slides.body: Turn recordings into interaction-quality scores you can defend.
slides.narration: So stop guessing, and start scoring the flow with FlowSense.
